column1: DEBBY 8 AM | Winds are up to 60 mph. Next track update is at 11 a.m. but I don't expect any changes. Steady movement north just offshore will keep the core of Debby in the Gulf. Updates right now LIVE on ABC Action News.

There is a young commercial fisherman that fell overboard down near Sanibel island. Coast Gaurd called off the search. Heart breaks thinking about him and his family. Storm

Just a thought...and I hope I don't sound like a "Debbie Downer". Many of us read the comments in order to get a better feel of what is going on, and how the storm is affecting others. Many people are trying to find information specific to their locale.
Would it not be better to have many less posts saying "Thanks, Denis" or inane questions or comments? I think Denis it makes it more difficult to find useful information. Denis and his team are incredible! I appreciate everything they do! I simply feel those of us in search of needed information would find it easier to find without so many comments that are not needed.
Stay safe, everyone!

there is nothing wrong with a prelim storm like this for the last minute season preppers to get it together. An additional tank for the grill, 10 gallons for the generator, and some grab and go food, was about all i needed. It was a good reminder for my husband to start the generator (make sure it's all good, last year we needed a new carburetor and you don't want to learn that in the middle of a disaster). So no hurricane shutters and putting stuff inside, but those last few things you need without stressing too much was probably good for some people. I still can't believe how many people are at lowes and home depot two days before a storm for plywood, I started asking people one time, hey, how long have you lived in Florida that you are doing this now?
Yeah just enough for Duke Energy to fail! Now that is my fear and not the storm!

Well Tony, I too have been here 30 years and I do have hurricane shutters and two generators. I live in a non-evac zone but we had one storm that took roof tiles off, and back yard flooding that came into the house. So it can happen, just the luck of the draw sometimes.
